hypothesis: If CompGen uses a chemically-informed autoencoder pretrained on Materials Project together with a conditional latent diffusion model conditioned on adsorbate identity, adsorption energy, and allowed elements, then it will generate shell-wise catalytic surface compositions that, compared to chemically-agnostic baselines, exhibit lower Fréchet Distance to target distributions, lower Leakage (better constraint compliance), and higher Cosine Similarity (better point-wise quality), with pretraining and LoRA fine-tuning improving overall performance and data efficiency on HEA datasets.
